commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- Initial tasks come from props; real-time updates from other team members arrive via stream
- Call the taskUpdate action when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

stream.description: Real-time task updates from other team members

taskChanged.description: A task was moved, edited, or created by another user — update it in the board

getBoardState.description: Current board state including any unsaved edits

issues.description: The '+ Add Task' trigger is a clickable Box instead of a Button, so it lacks guaranteed button interaction affordances (keyboard activation/focus behavior and standard hover/focus states). Replace it with a Button (or add explicit interactive hover/focus/keyboard handling).
issues.fix: The '+ Add Task' trigger is a clickable Box instead of a Button, so it lacks guaranteed button interaction affordances (keyboard activation/focus behavior and standard hover/focus states). Replace it with a Button (or add explicit interactive hover/focus/keyboard handling).

issues.description: Replace the clickable `Box as={Clickable}` used for “+ Add Task” (lines 353–369) with a semantic `Button` (or ensure it is keyboard-focusable with `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, and Enter/Space key handlers). As written, it may not be fully keyboard accessible.
issues.fix: Replace the clickable `Box as={Clickable}` used for “+ Add Task” (lines 353–369) with a semantic `Button` (or ensure it is keyboard-focusable with `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, and Enter/Space key handlers). As written, it may not be fully keyboard accessible.

issues.description: The task title double-click interaction on `Text as={Clickable}` (lines 230–236) is pointer-specific and not keyboard discoverable; add keyboard-trigger support (e.g., focusable button/text control with Enter/Space) or rely on a semantic edit button as the primary accessible trigger.
issues.fix: The task title double-click interaction on `Text as={Clickable}` (lines 230–236) is pointer-specific and not keyboard discoverable; add keyboard-trigger support (e.g., focusable button/text control with Enter/Space) or rely on a semantic edit button as the primary accessible trigger.

issues.description: No error state is shown if `taskUpdate` operations (move/edit/create) fail — add inline/toast error feedback and optionally rollback optimistic updates.
issues.fix: No error state is shown if `taskUpdate` operations (move/edit/create) fail — add inline/toast error feedback and optionally rollback optimistic updates.

evaluation.critique: The component is incomplete (code cuts off mid-function) and has critical issues: nearly all hardcoded values instead of design tokens (no var(--ggui-*) usage for colors, spacing, or sizes), missing visual hierarchy structure (no proper Card/Stack composition for columns), and lacks interactive polish (no hover states, transitions, or disabled feedback). The data presentation has some structure but cannot be fully evaluated due to truncation.
